questions: Were all code changes completed as specified in the implementation plan?
Do all tests pass including the new resolveTicket test suite?
Were any files modified beyond the expected scope?

answers.answer: Yes. resolveTicket() in src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts was updated with: (1) an optional fetchFn parameter for DI testability, (2) archived ticket fetch fallback with active-first priority for exact ID/short ID matches, (3) combined numeric ambiguity detection across both active and archived sets. One deviation from the plan: the active-first return was refined to only apply to exact ID/short ID matches, not numeric matches, to properly support cross-set numeric ambiguity detection (AC #9). For numeric refs, both sets are always fetched before deciding.
evidence: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:86-167 — Updated resolveTicket() with optional fetchFn param, archived fallback, and combined numeric check
npm test output — 51 tests pass (42 existing + 9 new), zero failures

answers.answer: Yes. All 51 tests pass: 42 pre-existing tests (matchTicket, extractTicketRef, flags, skill install) plus 9 new resolveTicket tests covering archived resolution by ID/shortId/number, active priority, missing ticket, cross-set ambiguity, archived-only regression, archived fetch error, and active fetch error.
evidence: resolveTicket test suite — all 9 test cases reported as passing

answers.answer: No. Only the 2 expected files were modified: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts and src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts. Boundary files (list.ts, latest.ts) and caller files (tickets/index.ts, comments/index.ts) were confirmed unmodified by direct inspection.
evidence: Direct file inspection confirmed src/tickets/index.ts, src/comments/index.ts, src/tickets/list.ts, src/tickets/latest.ts are unchanged
npm run build — successful compilation with no errors

statement: Implementation complete. resolveTicket() in src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts now searches both active and archived tickets. Active exact ID/short ID matches return immediately without an archived fetch. Numeric refs always check both sets for cross-set ambiguity. 9 new tests cover all acceptance criteria. All 51 tests pass, typecheck and build succeed, and only the 2 expected files were modified.
